summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: An example of a colloid is:

Milk is a colloid with fat droplets (and protein micelles) dispersed in water. Colloid particles (1–1000 nm) don't settle.

Example: Aerosol sprays are examples of which type of mixture?

Aerosols are colloids of liquid droplets (or solid particles) dispersed in gas — like hairspray or fog.

Example: Gelatin and Jell-O are examples of:

Gelatin is a colloid in which protein molecules form a network trapping water — a gel (solid dispersing medium with liquid dispersed phase).
